description: A vertical graphic styled like a shared news article/link card on a white background. The top is a full-color photograph of an auto-body collision-repair shop: a man wearing safety glasses and a white dust mask, in a dark long-sleeve shirt and gloves, kneels and uses a sander/polisher on the side panel of a vehicle, while a woman, also in safety glasses and a dust mask with her hair tied back, leans in over him to assist; a red tool chest is visible at left. Overlaid near the bottom of the photo is a white rounded banner with two logos: 'I-CAR' (blue, registered trademark) and, after a divider, 'APPRENTICESHIPUSA' (navy 'APPRENTICESHIP' + red 'USA') with three small stars and red/white/blue underline accents. Below the photo, large dark-grey sans-serif text reads: 'I-CAR launches two-year apprenticeship program for shops backed by Department of Labor.' The style is clean and minimal, mimicking a social link preview. The messaging promotes the administration's 'Make America Skilled Again' workforce theme by highlighting a DOL-backed private apprenticeship program. Target audience is the general public, the automotive-repair industry, and prospective apprentices.
notable_text: I-CAR | APPRENTICESHIPUSA - I-CAR launches two-year apprenticeship program for shops backed by Department of Labor
